description: The QuaternionMemberEditor component is a low-level component for use in editing FloatQ elements as XYZ Euler angles in degrees. It is used, for example, in the ProtoFlux input node for FloatQ values.

fields.Continuous: Whether changes update a value instantly.
fields._path: see Ref Hacking. essentially accesses a member underneath another one. for example, "x" will access the x element of a Float3, Float2, Float4, or FloatQ.
fields._target: Target field, which should be a FloatQ.
fields.Vertical: Whether the target UI is a vertical layout, as opposed to a horizontal layout.
fields._xDrive: Field to be driven by the X angle. Should be the ``Content`` field on the Text component that ``_xEditor`` is pointing to.
fields._yDrive: Field to be driven by the Y angle.
fields._zDrive: Field to be driven by the Z angle.
fields._xButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the ``_xEditor``
fields._yButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the ``_yEditor``
fields._zButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the ``_zEditor``
fields._editingValue: The value that the user is currently entering at it's current editing state. updates continuously.

methods.EditingStarted: Called when the component has started editing.
methods.EditingChanged: Called when the component's editing value changes.
methods.EditingFinished: Called when the component finishes editing.

usage: This component's behavior is very similar to PrimitiveMemberEditor, but a layer of abstraction exists between the FloatQ field in ``_target`` and the text field being driven for each member. This abstraction layer converts to and from Euler angles and quaterion values, which is then used for building a UI to edit quaternions as Euler angles.
